message:
fix(storage): land typed SqliteValue refactor — eliminates rows<2 bug in batch insert by removing the buggy cache entirely

Lands the in-flight type-safety refactor that was already staged in
the working tree (per AGENTS.md "treat as your own" rule for
multi-agent environments). The refactor incidentally fixes a real
release-build bug in cached_message_batch_insert_sql by removing the
function entirely.

The bug it removes:

cached_message_batch_insert_sql (added in dc4160af) guarded its row-
count input with `debug_assert!((2..=MESSAGE_INSERT_BATCH_SIZE)
.contains(&rows))`. debug_assert is compiled out of release builds,
so a release-build call with rows ∈ {0, 1} would index into the
pre-allocated Vec at slots that hold an empty String, return "" via
.get(rows).map(String::as_str).expect(...), and the caller would
hand "" to tx.execute_compat. The sole production caller had a
chunk.len() == 1 short-circuit so it never hit this path, but a
future caller without that guard would silently produce malformed
SQL. Fixing it in place would have been a defensive `assert!` upgrade,
but the cache itself was a micro-optimization (~600 chars of SQL
formatting, ~microseconds, dwarfed by the SQLite INSERT it precedes)
and removing it is cleaner.

What the refactor brings in:

(1) Switch the multi-row INSERT path from ParamValue (untyped) to
    SqliteValue (typed enum). The new path uses
    tx.execute_with_params(&sql, &sql_values) instead of the
    fparams!-style execute_compat. Each row's eight columns are now
    constructed via typed helpers:

    - sqlite_text_value(&str) -> SqliteValue::Text
    - sqlite_cow_text_value(Cow<'_, str>) -> SqliteValue::Text
      (handles both Cow::Borrowed and Cow::Owned inputs without
      cloning the underlying bytes)
    - sqlite_optional_text_value(Option<&str>) -> Text or Null
    - sqlite_optional_i64_value(Option<i64>) -> Integer or Null
    - sqlite_optional_blob_value(Option<Vec<u8>>) -> Blob or Null
    - sqlite_role_value(&MessageRole) -> Text matching the role enum

    Type-checked at the call site rather than relying on the
    fparams! macro to do the right thing for each value flavor.

(2) franken_insert_new_message (single-row path) gets the same
    treatment — explicit SqliteValue array passed to
    execute_with_params, so the single-row and multi-row paths now
    share identical column-binding semantics.

(3) Revert role_str_ref's &str return: replaced with sqlite_role_value
    (which returns SqliteValue::Text directly, no intermediate &str).
    role_str (the owned-String version) keeps its inline match arms
    rather than going through sqlite_role_value + into() conversions
    for callers that need an owned String.

(4) Drop the OnceLock<Vec<String>> cache (MESSAGE_BATCH_INSERT_SQL)
    and the cached_message_batch_insert_sql helper. The SQL is now
    formatted on each batch call. For 100-message batches the
    placeholder string is ~800 bytes and one allocation; this is
    rounding error compared to the 8-column typed Bind + actual
    INSERT execution. Verified `cargo check` and `cargo check --tests`
    both pass clean.

Net diff: -72 lines, +85 lines. cached_message_batch_insert_sql,
MESSAGE_BATCH_INSERT_SQL, and the OnceLock import are all removed;
six new typed-helper functions added next to path_to_string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
